name: IP Addressing & Subnetting (CIDR)
description: Subnetting divides an address block to fit host requirements: from the needed host count the required host bits and prefix length are computed, the block is split into subnets, and if a subnet is too small more host bits are borrowed; network and broadcast addresses are reserved and routes are aggregated with CIDR.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Network address block"]
    N2["Determine host count"]
    N3["Compute required bits"]
    N4["Set subnet mask / prefix"]
    N5["Split into subnets"]
    N6{"Enough addresses?"}
    N7["Borrow more host bits"]
    N8["Reserve net + broadcast"]
    N9["Usable host range"]
    N10["Aggregate routes (CIDR)"]
    N11["Subnet plan"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|no| N7
    N7 -->|recompute| N3
    N6 -->|yes| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 --> N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Classless Inter-domain Routing (CIDR): The Internet Address Assignment and Aggregation Plan (RFC 4632)
Computer Networks (5th ed.)
sources.authors: Tanenbaum, A. S.; Wetherall, D. J.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.

flowchartStandard.name: source_grounded_extraction_v2
flowchartStandard.sourceGrounding: Nodes, decisions, branches, and loops were extracted from the cited sources; each node carries a sourceRef to its supporting citation. Awaiting human spot-check before verified=true.
